Empires & Puzzles Yunan Review: Is the Sand King Still Royal?

Remember when Yunan was the scariest tank in the game? Neither do we, it's been that long. We dust off the Sand King to see if his healing reduction and sand damage can still scratch the surface of the current meta, or if he's just building castles in the sky.
Yunan: The King of Sand (and Snail-Paced Mana)

Yunan is a relic from a simpler time in Empires & Puzzles. As the "King of the Sand Empire," he was once a formidable tank that players dreaded facing. A 5-star Nature hero with a Slow mana speed, his kit is designed around attrition—wearing enemies down with Sand damage and preventing them from healing it back. This review focuses on his
, a hero who has seen kingdoms rise and fall while he slowly charges his mana. He’s the kind of hero who takes so long to get ready, the battle is usually over before he arrives.

Yunan's design is classic high-fantasy with a Middle Eastern flair. He stands stoic in golden armor, a turban, and a heavy cloak, gripping a sword that looks like it weighs more than most 3-star heroes. The art captures his role as a sturdy, unmovable object. Unlike his "Baywatch" costume, this version commands a certain regal respect, even if his stats don't anymore. He looks like he’s about to deliver a profound monologue, which is fitting given how long you have to wait for his special skill.
Let’s be blunt: Yunan’s base stats are ancient history. With a maxed attack of

, he is statistically invisible compared to modern heroes who regularly boast 1300+ in both categories. His stats were designed for a game where 700 power was considered "god-tier." In the current meta, his bulk is non-existent, and his attack stat is so low that his direct damage is negligible. He relies entirely on his DoT (Damage over Time) to be relevant.
"Sand Storm" is a classic attrition skill. It does three things: hits everyone, applies a DoT (Sand Damage), and reduces healing received.
200% to all enemies. With his pitiful attack stat, this is essentially a "tickle" to modern heroes.
The DoT is his main source of pressure. It bypasses defense stats, which is good, but it can be cleansed.
The -50% healing reduction is his most unique utility. It prevents enemies from recovering, which synergizes well with the DoT.
. In a team game, being selfish with the cleanse is a major drawback, especially for a Slow hero.
Slow. He requires 12 tiles to charge. With a level 29 mana troop, you can get this down, but he will always be lagging behind the pace of modern battles.
With ~618 attack, a 200% hit deals very little damage to enemies with 1200+ defense. The bulk of his damage comes from the Sand DoT (381 over 3 turns), which averages to
. This is dangerously low when modern heroes have 2500+ HP.
Reducing healing by 50% effectively halves the value of enemy healers. Against a 1000 HP heal, you deny 500 HP.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Sand Empire family bonus and his passives are largely outdated but still offer some flavor.

The family bonus offers a defense boost (10%/20%) and a boost to healing received. It’s a nice little layer of survivability, but given how fragile his base stats are, 10% doesn't save him from being one-shot by a modern sniper.
Yunan does not possess modern passive skills like "start battle with mana" or "resist ailments," which further hurts his viability.
Start the battle with a small amount of mana. For a Slow hero, this is helpful, but usually not enough to make him fire before the enemy team decimates yours.
In 2018 and early 2019, Yunan was a top-tier tank. His high defense (for the time) and punishing DoT made him a nightmare in Alliance Wars. He was a staple in the green tank meta.
Power creep hasn't just crept up on Yunan; it has sprinted past him, lapped him, and is now drinking a smoothie at the finish line. His stats are nearly half of what modern heroes offer.

There is almost no scenario in high-level play where base Yunan is viable. He is too slow, too weak, and his utility is easily countered by cleansers. He effectively serves as a placeholder for his costume.

The costume is superior in almost every way. The base version is strictly for nostalgia or extremely niche situations where you desperately need healing reduction and have no other option.

-50% healing is a strong debuff that can cripple reliance on heavy healers.

He can cleanse ailments from himself, ensuring his DoT keeps ticking if he fires.
Slow mana is a death sentence in the current Fast/Very Fast meta.
His base stats are essentially those of a modern 3-star or weak 4-star hero.
His primary damage source (DoT) and debuff can be cleansed by a 4-star Rigard.
Base Yunan only cleanses himself, leaving his team to suffer.
The only place he shines. When his speed is set to Very Fast, his devastating skill can actually fire.
Similar to Rush Tournaments, he can be a nuisance here.
His DoT is useful for chipping away at bosses with high defense.
Yunan essentially needs a team that can keep him alive or speed him up.
Mother North - Yunan - Alfrike. In Rush wars, this wall of meat can be hard to break.
Pair him with heroes like Ludwig or Xnolphod to force his mana to charge.
Rigard, Vivica, and modern cleansers completely nullify his kit.
Proteus, Hel, or any mana cutter will ensure he never fires.
Their Stoneskin ignores his direct damage (though DoT still helps).

If you pulled Yunan early, congratulations, you have a 5-star! He will serve you well in map stages and lower-level raids as a tank. Do not prioritize him over modern 4-star heroes who are faster and more versatile.

He is a Soul Exchange token. Unless you have his costume and plan to use him in Rush Wars exclusively, he is not worth the tonics or emblems. The resource cost to make him "okay" is too high compared to the return.
"He used to be the king of tanks, now he's just a sandcastle waiting for the tide."
"His animation is slower than his mana speed."
"Only useful for the senseless 'very fast' events."
- A forum user summing up his modern niche.

Yunan is a king without a kingdom. The game has evolved past the slow, attrition-based warfare he excelled in. While his costume gives him a second wind as a lifeguard, the base version is drowning in the deep end of the hero pool. Keep him if you love the art or need a Rush War tank, but don't expect him to rule the desert like he used to.
A dusty relic best left in the tomb unless you have his costume or a specific Rush War strategy.
A detailed 2026 review of Yunan, the 5-star Nature hero from the Sand Empire family. We analyze his stats, special skill, and viability in the modern era of Empires & Puzzles.
